params.name: Kurgan Stelae
params.description:
On Feb 6, 1213 AD, a huge stone statue is planted in the ground.
Kurgans are one of the main constituents of the Central Asian ethnic groups and are now spread across more than a dozen countries and regions worldwide. According to historical records, the Kurgens have unique burial ceremonies and customs. When a person dies, their body is placed in a felt tent. Their descendants and relatives slaughter horses and sheep and place them in front of the tent for sacrifice. They lead horses around …
